category_title: High-engagement primary students who value instructional support and a quiet environment
minimal_context: The student is sitting in a comfy blue chair in the intervention room, working on building words with letter combinations before rereading a leveled text.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Let's start our phonics drill by building some words with these letters, and then we will reread our leveled text.

STUDENT: Okay. I like sitting in these comfy blue chairs. It’s really quiet in the intervention room.

TUTOR: Yes, it is. Go ahead and try building the first word.

STUDENT: (Moves a few letters around) I don't know this one. I need getting help from the teacher so I don't have to wait a long time for help.

TUTOR: What do you think the next letter is? Just keep trying your best to figure it out.

STUDENT: (Becomes somewhat less engaged and shows distractibility) I can't do it. (Stops looking at the letters and starts playing with the fabric on the chair instead).

options.A: Purposefully select LLI-leveled texts written by and/or featuring people of color for the student to read during the intervention.
options.B: Regularly monitor the student's motivation specific to the intervention and modify instruction in accordance with the findings.
options.C: Provide the student with opportunities to voice their opinions and ensure those opinions are valued during dialogic reading pedagogy.
options.D: Transition the decision of when and for how long to take quiet time in the intervention room from the tutor to the student.
